title: Terminal-sensitive softmax suite
hypothesis: The fused terminal-output path should continue to improve latency and energy on a small terminal-sensitive softmax-tail model slice once the comparison is staged as corrected-contract reference measurement first and paired fused-vs-non-fused comparison second.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does fused terminal output improve a corrected-contract non-fused nm1 reference across a small terminal-sensitive softmax-tail model slice?
include: measurement-only non-fused nm1 reference points on a small terminal-sensitive softmax-tail suite
paired fused vs non-fused nm1 comparison on the same suite
exclude: broad architecture sweep before the per-model fused comparison is established
follow_on_broad_sweep: run a broader ranking sweep only if the focused per-model fused comparisons are positive or mixed enough to justify reintegration

expected_benefit: tests whether the fused win generalizes beyond the original tiny proof
uses the new measurement-only evaluation mode to keep first-stage evidence clean
reduces the risk of reopening broad ranking questions too early
stays inside the currently supported ONNX lowering subset instead of opening a larger model-support item first
risks: the chosen models may still be too small or too similar to the original proof
using locally generated softmax-tail graphs may prove terminal sensitivity without yet covering truly different operator families
a later non-MLP expansion may still require a separate lowering-support item

expected_result.reason: this first item only records corrected-contract non-fused reference metrics for the selected terminal-sensitive suite
the fused path is expected to beat the corrected non-fused references on at least part of the selected terminal-sensitive suite
